agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the product catalog, shopping cart, checkout, payment, promotions, and post-purchase lifecycle. The domain enforces inventory integrity, pricing fairness, and order immutability after payment.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A transient collection of line items a customer intends to purchase. No inventory is reserved at this stage."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated when a customer commits to purchasing cart contents. Triggers inventory reservation."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-bound hold on inventory units, created when checkout begins, preventing other customers from purchasing the same units. Expires after 15 minutes if payment is not completed."},{"term":"Abandoned Checkout","definition":"A checkout session where no payment confirmation is received within the 15-minute reservation window."},{"term":"Promotion","definition":"A seller-defined pricing adjustment applied to a specific product (e.g., seasonal sale, flash deal). A product may have at most one active promotion."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A buyer-supplied code that reduces order cost. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing on the same order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmation","definition":"The irreversible event marking that funds have been captured. After this point, order contents are immutable."},{"term":"Order","definition":"The finalized record of a purchase, created upon payment confirmation. Cannot be modified post-payment."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-calendar-day period after delivery during which a customer may request a refund."},{"term":"Listed Product","definition":"A product visible in the catalog. Visibility does not imply purchasability — zero-inventory listed products cannot be bought."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Inventory is not reserved when items are added to the cart; reservation occurs only when checkout begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Premature reservation would corrupt inventory counts and block other customers from purchasing available stock. Cart is a non-committal intent; checkout is the commitment boundary.","testable_assertion":"Adding an item to a cart does not decrement available inventory or create any reservation record."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An inventory reservation must be released if payment is not confirmed within 15 minutes of checkout initiation.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy. The release mechanism itself is invariant (reservations must eventually expire), but the specific timeout is a tunable policy a VP could change.","testable_assertion":"A reservation created at time T with no payment confirmation is released at T+15 minutes, and the reserved units are returned to available inventory."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product can have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on the same product creates ambiguity in which price applies, leading to pricing corruption and potential revenue loss. This is a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Attempting to activate a second promotion on a product that already has an active promotion must fail or deactivate the existing one before activating the new one."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive — only one discount mechanism applies per order.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Allowing both to stack could result in below-cost pricing and violates the business's pricing integrity. The system must enforce a single discount path per order.","testable_assertion":"When a product in the order has an active promotion, applying a discount code must be rejected (or vice versa). The order total must reflect exactly one discount mechanism or none."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"An order cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment modification would break financial reconciliation, fulfillment integrity, and audit trails. This is a state integrity constraint — once the Order aggregate transitions to Paid, its line items are frozen.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to add, remove, or change line items on an order with a confirmed payment must be rejected by the system."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling non-existent inventory creates fulfillment failures, backorder liabilities, and customer dissatisfaction. Listing is a catalog concern; purchasability is an inventory concern. They are decoupled but inventory is a hard gate.","testable_assertion":"Attempting to begin checkout (or complete purchase) with a product whose available inventory is zero must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days of the delivery date.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a business policy that could be extended for VIP customers, holiday seasons, or regulatory changes. The rule that a refund window exists is invariant; the specific duration is policy.","testable_assertion":"A refund request submitted on delivery_date + 31 days must be rejected. A refund request submitted on delivery_date + 30 days must be accepted (assuming other conditions are met)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"The customer adds another item to the cart","then":"Available inventory for all cart items remains unchanged and no reservation records exist"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer has items in their cart and all items have available inventory","when":"The customer initiates checkout","then":"Inventory reservations are created for each line item, and available inventory is decremented by the reserved quantities"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A checkout was initiated 15 minutes ago and payment has not been confirmed","when":"The reservation timeout elapses","then":"All inventory reservations for that checkout session are released and available inventory is restored to pre-checkout levels"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A checkout was initiated and payment is confirmed within 15 minutes","when":"Payment confirmation is received","then":"The reservations are converted into a confirmed order and inventory is permanently decremented"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An administrator attempts to create a second promotion for the same product","then":"The system rejects the new promotion (or requires deactivation of the existing one first)"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"A customer's cart contains a product with an active promotion applied","when":"The customer attempts to apply a discount code at checkout","then":"The system rejects the discount code and informs the customer that promotional pricing and discount codes cannot be combined"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A customer's cart contains a product without any active promotion","when":"The customer applies a valid discount code at checkout","then":"The discount code is applied and the order total reflects the code's discount"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"An order has a confirmed payment","when":"Any actor attempts to modify the order's line items","then":"The system rejects the modification and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A product is listed in the catalog with zero available inventory","when":"A customer attempts to add it to cart and proceed to checkout","then":"The system prevents purchase and displays an out-of-stock indication"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered 29 days ago","when":"The customer submits a refund request","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An order was delivered 31 days ago","when":"The customer submits a refund request","then":"The refund request is rejected with a message indicating the refund window has expired"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A customer is in checkout with reserved inventory","when":"Another customer attempts to purchase the same item and no unreserved inventory remains","then":"The second customer is informed the item is unavailable"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Customer adds item to cart, item goes to zero inventory before checkout begins","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation must fail with an out-of-stock error. The cart may still show the item but the system must validate inventory at checkout start."},{"scenario":"Two customers initiate checkout simultaneously for the last unit of a product","expected_behavior":"Only one reservation succeeds. The second customer receives an out-of-stock error. Reservation must be atomic and race-condition safe."},{"scenario":"Payment confirmation arrives at exactly the 15-minute mark","expected_behavior":"Boundary must be well-defined: either inclusive (payment at exactly T+15 is accepted) or exclusive. The spec is ambiguous on this — recommend inclusive (≤15 minutes)."},{"scenario":"Active promotion is deactivated mid-checkout after price was shown to customer","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout initiation should be honored. Changing price mid-checkout violates customer trust and may violate consumer protection laws."},{"scenario":"Discount code applied to order with mix of promoted and non-promoted products","expected_behavior":"The spec says one discount mechanism per order — clarify whether the discount code can apply to only the non-promoted items, or if the entire order is blocked from discount codes if any item has a promotion."},{"scenario":"Refund requested for partially delivered order (some items delivered, some not)","expected_behavior":"The 30-day window should start from the delivery date of each individual item, or from the final delivery. Spec is ambiguous."},{"scenario":"Product inventory restored (e.g., restock) while customer views out-of-stock message","expected_behavior":"Customer should be able to purchase on retry. Real-time inventory visibility is ideal but eventual consistency is acceptable."},{"scenario":"Checkout abandoned at 14 minutes, customer returns at 16 minutes","expected_behavior":"Reservation was released at 15 minutes. Customer must re-initiate checkout, which will attempt a new reservation and may fail if inventory is now zero."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect inventory reservation logic can lead to overselling (fulfillment costs, refunds, brand damage) or underselling (lost revenue from unnecessary stock-outs). Promotion stacking bugs can cause below-cost sales. The 30-day refund window bounds financial liability exposure.","operational":"The 15-minute reservation timeout directly impacts checkout conversion rates — too short loses sales, too long blocks inventory. Promotion uniqueness constraint simplifies pricing engine logic and prevents audit complexity. Order immutability post-payment simplifies fulfillment pipeline.","user":"Customers expect cart items to be available at checkout — the gap between cart and reservation creates a window of disappointment. Clear messaging around promotion vs. discount code exclusivity prevents confusion. Refund window clarity sets expectations and reduces support tickets.","risk":"Race conditions on inventory reservation are the highest technical risk. Failure to release abandoned reservations is the highest operational risk (phantom stock-outs). Allowing post-payment modification is a compliance and financial reconciliation risk.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Must receive clear feedback on inventory availability, pricing rules, and refund eligibility. Poor UX around mutual exclusivity of discounts will drive cart abandonment."},{"group":"Product/Merchandising Team","impact":"One-promotion-per-product constraint affects how campaigns are planned. Cannot run overlapping promotions on the same SKU."},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability post-payment simplifies reconciliation. Refund window bounds liability. Discount stacking prevention protects margins."},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Inventory reservation accuracy directly impacts pick/pack/ship reliability. Overselling creates exception handling burden."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Clear refund window policy reduces dispute volume. Discount code rejection messages must be clear enough to deflect support tickets."}]},"confidence":{"level":72,"reasoning":"The spec is well-articulated for core rules but leaves several edge cases ambiguous: (1) whether discount code exclusivity applies per-line-item or per-order when orders contain a mix of promoted and non-promoted items, (2) boundary behavior at exactly 15 minutes, (3) whether the refund window is per-item or per-order for partial deliveries, (4) what happens to price if promotion changes mid-checkout. No contradictions detected, but these ambiguities prevent full confidence.","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory is not reserved at cart time — clear and unambiguous","Order immutability after payment — clear state transition boundary","One active promotion per product — clear cardinality constraint","Zero inventory blocks purchase regardless of listing status — clear decoupling of catalog from inventory"],"low_confidence_areas":["Discount code + promotion mutual exclusivity granularity (per-order vs per-line-item)","Exact boundary behavior of 15-minute reservation timeout (inclusive or exclusive)","Refund window start date for multi-shipment orders","Price lock semantics during checkout when promotions change","Whether BR-6 check happens at cart-add, checkout-start, or both"],"assumptions":["The 15-minute timeout is measured from checkout initiation, not from the last user interaction","Discount code vs promotion exclusivity applies at the order level as stated, not per line item","Refund window is calculated from the final delivery date of the complete order","Zero-inventory check occurs at minimum at checkout initiation, possibly also at cart-add as a courtesy"]},"escalations":[{"type":"ambiguity","description":"The spec states discount codes cannot be combined with promotional pricing per order, but does not address orders containing a mix of promoted and non-promoted products. Should the discount code apply to only non-promoted line items, or is the entire order blocked from discount codes if any single item has a promotion?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Product/Merchandising Team","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["A: Block discount codes entirely if any item in the order has a promotion","B: Allow discount codes to apply only to non-promoted line items in the same order"],"recommendation":"Option A is safer and simpler to implement and audit, but Option B is more customer-friendly. Recommend clarifying with the product team. Option A is the conservative default."},{"type":"ambiguity","description":"Refund window of 30 days from delivery is ambiguous for orders with multiple shipments. Does the clock start from the first delivery, the last delivery, or per-item?","affected_stakeholders":["Customers","Customer Support","Finance/Accounting"],"options":["A: 30 days from last item delivered","B: 30 days per individual item from its delivery date","C: 30 days from first item delivered"],"recommendation":"Option B (per-item) is most customer-friendly and industry-standard for multi-shipment orders. Recommend confirming with product and legal."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
